---
title: "Soundscapy tutorials"
---

Each tutorial demonstrates a different aspect of soundscape analysis with Soundscapy.

If you are new to soundscape analysis, start with [About Soundscape Analysis](background.qmd) for an overview of the core concepts before diving into code.

## Where to start

| I want to... | Go to |
| --- | --- |
| Understand the concepts behind the analysis | [About Soundscape Analysis](background.qmd) |
| Run something immediately | [Quick Start](QuickStart.qmd) |
| Learn step by step, new to Python | [Getting Started](0_QuickStart_for_Beginners.qmd) |
| See the analytical approach behind the library | [Analysing and Representing Soundscapes](HowToAnalyseAndRepresentSoundscapes.qmd) |
| Work through a full assessment step by step | Tutorials 1–6 below, in order |
| Analyse audio recordings | [Binaural Audio Analysis](BinauralAnalysis.qmd) — requires `soundscapy[audio]` |
| Use the Soundscape Perception Index or CircE | [Soundscape Perception Index](4_Understanding_Soundscape_Perception_Index.qmd) — requires `soundscapy[r]` |

## Tutorials

1. [Understanding Soundscape Analysis](1_Understanding_Soundscape_Analysis.qmd) — ISO 12913 framework, the circumplex model, and PAQ data
2. [Working with Survey Data](2_Working_with_Soundscape_Survey_Data.qmd) — loading, validating, and transforming PAQ data
3. [Visualization Techniques](3_Advanced_Visualization_Techniques.qmd) — scatter plots, density plots, joint plots, multi-panel figures
4. [Soundscape Perception Index](4_Understanding_Soundscape_Perception_Index.qmd) — fitting MSN distributions and scoring against target distributions
5. [Working with Databases](5_Working_with_Soundscape_Databases.qmd) — ISD, ARAUS, SATP, and language-specific coordinates
6. [Complete Assessment Workflow](6_Soundscape_Assessment_Tutorial.qmd) — end-to-end assessment of a custom dataset
